title: Favourites Sort Order — add 'recently added first' toggle to the favourites list
commit_message: feat: add TOGGLE_FAVOURITES_SORT action and sortOrder field to favouritesReducer — sort by modelId desc when 'recent'
changed_symbols: toggleFavouritesSort (new action creator — returns { type: TOGGLE_FAVOURITES_SORT })

favouritesReducer (modified — initial state gains sortOrder: 'api'; new TOGGLE_FAVOURITES_SORT case toggles 'api'↔'recent'; UPDATEALLFAVOURITIESDETAILS now sorts by modelId desc when sortOrder === 'recent')

what_the_diff_does.before: favouritesReducer stores userFavourites in API order. No sort state exists. The UI displays favourites in whatever order the backend returns them.
what_the_diff_does.after: favouritesReducer gains a sortOrder field ('api' by default). TOGGLE_FAVOURITES_SORT flips between 'api' and 'recent'. When 'recent', UPDATEALLFAVOURITIESDETAILS sorts the incoming items by modelId descending (highest ID = most recently added appears first). A sort toggle button in the Favourites screen dispatches toggleFavouritesSort().
what_the_diff_does.visible_change: Favourites screen shows a sort toggle. In 'recently added' mode, the most recently favourited item appears at the top instead of the bottom.
what_the_diff_does.compile_note: UPDATEALLFAVOURITIESDETAILS is backward-compatible: when sortOrder is 'api' (the default), items are stored exactly as the API returned them — no change in behaviour for existing callers.

CRITICAL.reason: Two files contribute CRITICAL tests: (1) favourites-watchlist.feature.test.js — describe('favouritesReducer') matches the changed symbol, 8 tests CRITICAL. (2) favourites-sort.feature.test.js — declares favouritesReducer.js, actions.js, and actiotypes.js as sources and contains describe('TOGGLE_FAVOURITES_SORT') 2 tests, describe('toggleFavouritesSort') 3 tests, describe('favouritesReducer — sortOrder') 7 tests — all 12 are CRITICAL. Total CRITICAL: 20.
tests: favouritesReducer > starts with an empty userFavourites list
favouritesReducer > starts with favouritesFetched as null
favouritesReducer > UPDATEALLFAVOURITIESDETAILS replaces the entire favourites list
favouritesReducer > sets favouritesFetched to true once the list is successfully loaded
favouritesReducer > sets favouritesFetched to false when load fails (empty list)
favouritesReducer > replaces a populated list with an empty list when dispatched again
favouritesReducer > reflects the exact number of items provided in the action
favouritesReducer > returns unchanged state for an unknown action type

HIGH.reason: Two files qualify as HIGH: (1) action-creators.pure.test.js declares src/reducer/actions.js as a source — actions.js is changed. (2) The 16 non-CRITICAL tests in favourites-watchlist.feature.test.js (the toastReducer describe block) share the file whose sources include favouritesReducer.js and actiotypes.js — both changed.

SKIP.reason: All remaining test files do not declare actiotypes.js, actions.js, or favouritesReducer.js as a source.

coverage_gaps.gap: Covered by 2 tests in describe('TOGGLE_FAVOURITES_SORT') in favourites-sort.feature.test.js.
Covered by 3 tests in describe('toggleFavouritesSort') in favourites-sort.feature.test.js.
Covered by 7 tests in describe('favouritesReducer — sortOrder') in favourites-sort.feature.test.js.

jest_run_note: favourites-sort.feature.test.js runs 12 CRITICAL tests. favourites-watchlist.feature.test.js runs 8 CRITICAL + 16 HIGH tests. action-creators.pure.test.js adds 40 HIGH tests. All 3 gaps closed.
